{"id":"project-LICHEN-6aov","title":"RPL lollipop counter version comparison incomplete","description":"File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py:42-52

The version_is_newer() function uses a simplified modular comparison but doesn't implement the full RFC 6550 Section 7.2 lollipop counter semantics.

RFC 6550 defines DODAGVersionNumber as a lollipop counter with:
- Linear region (0-127): values in this region are always newer than values in the circular region
- Circular region (128-255): uses window-based modular comparison

Current code only does window-based modular comparison:
```python
diff = (new_version - old_version) % _VERSION_MODULUS
return 0 < diff < _VERSION_HALF
```

This fails when comparing across regions:
- If new_version=5 (linear) and old_version=250 (circular), the linear value should be newer
- But: (5-250)%256 = 11, and 0 < 11 < 128 is True, which happens to be correct
- If new_version=130 (circular) and old_version=10 (linear), linear should be newer
- But: (130-10)%256 = 120, and 0 < 120 < 128 is True, incorrectly claiming circular is newer

Impact: DODAG version transitions between linear/circular regions may be mishandled, causing nodes to accept stale DODAG versions or reject valid newer versions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:04.784718Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:04.784746Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-6aov","title":"RPL lollipop counter version comparison incomplete","description":"File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py:42-52

The version_is_newer() function uses a simplified modular comparison but doesn't implement the full RFC 6550 Section 7.2 lollipop counter semantics.

RFC 6550 defines DODAGVersionNumber as a lollipop counter with:
- Linear region (0-127): values in this region are always newer than values in the circular region
- Circular region (128-255): uses window-based modular comparison

Current code only does window-based modular comparison:
```python
diff = (new_version - old_version) % _VERSION_MODULUS
return 0 < diff < _VERSION_HALF
```

This fails when comparing across regions:
- If new_version=5 (linear) and old_version=250 (circular), the linear value should be newer
- But: (5-250)%256 = 11, and 0 < 11 < 128 is True, which happens to be correct
- If new_version=130 (circular) and old_version=10 (linear), linear should be newer
- But: (130-10)%256 = 120, and 0 < 120 < 128 is True, incorrectly claiming circular is newer

Impact: DODAG version transitions between linear/circular regions may be mishandled, causing nodes to accept stale DODAG versions or reject valid newer versions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:23:04.784718Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:21:00.217007242Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:21:00.217007242Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:26:00.217007242Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:21:00.217007242Z"}
